input:
May rang no bells. The dogs of hesychasm breezed through the hall past cameras and lasers, flinging music from the stand. We hauled the hope chest across a swelling and tipping pier, every heartbeat a plank above the churn. Feelers folded up in Casimir spaces beneath the cannonade of her seashell eyes, her brow gliding through the pastel morning. Have we passed Utnapishtim's station? Where was salvation? Lord Jesus Christ, have mercy on me, a sinner.

Time heals all wounds, exposed, pussing, doused in alcohol, hazy faces hovering to put protective instincts and aggression to bed. "What news?" says the beach mouse, grateful for the shoulder check, tucked into the forbidden desert, hoping it will all be benign.
